identity.name: Student
identity.definition: A Student is an individual learner enrolled in one or more educational organizations who engages in instructional activities, completes assessments and assignments, and serves as the primary anchor for all learning-related data, progress tracking, and outcomes across Renaissance products.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Student appears in grids and dashboards

attributes.description: Which school the student is enrolled at.

attributes.description: Key metric for AR, myON, Star Reading. Updated on assessment completion.

attributes.name: Math Level
attributes.description: Key metric for Freckle, Star Math. Updated on assessment/practice.

attributes.name: Enrollment Status
attributes.description: Active, Inactive, or Transferred. Controls visibility and roster membership.

attributes.name: Full Name
attributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears everywhere a Student is shown.

attributes.name: Grade Level
attributes.description: Determines age-appropriate content, benchmarks, and assessments. PreK-12, UG.

attributes.name: Student ID
attributes.description: Unique system identifier. Used for rostering, data matching, and SIS sync.

attributes.name: School
attributes.description: Which school the student is enrolled at.

attributes.name: Class(es)
attributes.description: Which classes the student belongs to.

attributes.name: Reading Level
attributes.description: Key metric for AR, myON, Star Reading. Updated on assessment completion.

attributes.name: Math Level
attributes.description: Key metric for Freckle, Star Math. Updated on assessment/practice.

attributes.name: Date of Birth
attributes.description: Used for age calculations, COPPA compliance. Visible to Admin only.

attributes.name: Username / Login
attributes.description: How the student authenticates. Unique within org.

attributes.name: Enrollment Status
attributes.description: Active, Inactive, Transferred. Controls visibility. Default: Active.

attributes.name: Demographic Data
attributes.description: Ethnicity, gender, ELL status, IEP status. For disaggregated reporting.

attributes.name: Guardian/Parent Contact
attributes.description: Email, phone. For parent-facing features.

attributes.name: State/District Student ID
attributes.description: External identifier for state reporting and cross-system matching.

attributes.name: Student Avatar
attributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears everywhere a Student is shown.

attributes.name: Language Preference
attributes.description: Primary language. Relevant for Lalilo, ELL accommodations. Default: English.

stories.title: View Student Profile
stories.benefit: I can understand their current reading and math levels, recent assessment scores, and assigned work.
stories.whenClause: I navigate to a Student from my class roster,
stories.thenClause: their key attributes, recent Scores, current Assignments, and Skill proficiency at a glance.

stories.title: Enroll Student in Class
stories.benefit: the student appears on the teacher's roster and can receive class-level assignments and assessments.
stories.whenClause: I add a student to a class,
stories.thenClause: the student should immediately appear in the class roster and inherit any active class-level assignments.

stories.title: Transfer Student
stories.benefit: their historical data follows them and they appear in the correct school's reports.
stories.thenClause: all historical scores and progress data move with them, and they are removed from their previous school's active roster.

businessRules.title: Uniqueness
businessRules.description: A Student is uniquely identified within a District by their Student ID. State Student IDs provide cross-district matching.

businessRules.title: Creation
businessRules.description: Students are primarily created via SIS roster sync (OneRoster, Clever, ClassLink). Manual creation requires at minimum: First Name, Last Name, Grade Level.

businessRules.title: Enrollment
businessRules.description: A Student must be enrolled in at least one School to be active. They may belong to zero or more Classes.

businessRules.title: Data Privacy
businessRules.description: Student PII (date of birth, demographics) is restricted to Admin roles. Teachers see name, grade, and academic data. FERPA and COPPA compliance required.

businessRules.title: Transfer
businessRules.description: When a Student transfers schools, historical Scores and progress data travel with them. The student is removed from their previous school's active roster but historical records persist.

businessRules.title: Deactivation
businessRules.description: Deactivated students are hidden from active rosters and reports but their data is retained for historical reporting and can be reactivated.

businessRules.title: Score Accumulation
businessRules.description: Scores are append-only. Once generated, a Score cannot be deleted (only excluded from certain reports by Admins).

states.name: Created
states.description: Initial state upon import or manual creation

states.name: Transferred
states.triggers: SIS sync or admin action; temporary state

states.name: Inactive
states.description: No longer participating but data retained

relationships.mechanics: Students are enrolled in Classes primarily via SIS roster sync (automated). Admins can also manually add/remove students.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Student can belong to multiple Classes; a Class has multiple Students. Typical: 1-8 classes per student, 15-35 students per class.
relationships.sorts: Students within a Class: alphabetical by last name (default), by grade level, by reading/math level.
relationships.filters: By grade level, by enrollment status (active/inactive), by demographic group, by proficiency level.
relationships.dependencies: If a Class is archived, students remain in the system but lose their class association for that class. Historical data persists.

relationships.mechanics: Scores are system-generated when a Student completes an Assessment. Auto-calculated by the assessment engine.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A Student accumulates many Scores over time; each Score belongs to exactly one Student.
relationships.sorts: By date (most recent first, default), by assessment type, by subject area, by score value.
relationships.filters: By assessment type (Star, DnA, FastBridge), by subject (Reading, Math, SEL), by date range, by benchmark category.
relationships.dependencies: If a Student is deactivated, Scores persist for historical reporting but are excluded from active aggregations.

relationships.mechanics: Students are assigned to a School via SIS roster sync. Transfer is handled by District Admins or system sync.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one (at a given time). A Student belongs to one School; a School contains many Students.
relationships.sorts: Students within a School: alphabetical (default), by grade level.
relationships.filters: By grade, by class, by enrollment status, by teacher.
relationships.dependencies: If a School is deactivated, students must be transferred to another School or deactivated.

nestedObjects.name: Assessment
nestedObjects.description: Teacher/admin schedules for student or class

nestedObjects.name: Assignment
nestedObjects.description: Teacher assigns to student directly or via class

nestedObjects.name: Skill
nestedObjects.description: System-calculated from assessment and practice data

allAttributes.name: Class(es)

allAttributes.name: Reading Level
allAttributes.description: Key metric for AR, myON, Star Reading. Updated on assessment completion.

allAttributes.name: Math Level
allAttributes.description: Key metric for Freckle, Star Math. Updated on assessment/practice.

allAttributes.name: Reading Proficiency
allAttributes.description: Current proficiency band for reading, derived from the student's most recent Star Reading score. Bands are configured at the district level.

allAttributes.name: Math Proficiency
allAttributes.description: Current proficiency band for math, derived from the student's most recent Star Math score. Bands are configured at the district level.

allAttributes.name: Number of Assignments
allAttributes.description: Count of active assignments currently assigned to this student. Includes all statuses except Deleted.

allAttributes.name: Enrollment Status
allAttributes.description: Active, Inactive, Transferred, Graduated. Controls visibility and roster membership.

allAttributes.name: Date of Birth
allAttributes.description: Used for age calculations, COPPA compliance. Visible to Admin only.

allAttributes.name: Username / Login
allAttributes.description: How the student authenticates. Unique within org.

allAttributes.name: Demographic Data
allAttributes.description: Ethnicity, gender, ELL status, IEP status. For disaggregated reporting.

allAttributes.name: State/District Student ID
allAttributes.description: External identifier for state reporting and cross-system matching.

allAttributes.name: Student Avatar
allAttributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears everywhere a Student is shown.

allAttributes.name: Language Preference
allAttributes.description: Primary language. Relevant for Lalilo, ELL accommodations. Default: English.

allCTAs.name: View Profile
allCTAs.description: Opens the full student record showing identity, enrollment, academic levels, and performance history.

allCTAs.name: Search / Filter
allCTAs.description: Find students within a list by name, grade, proficiency level, or enrollment status.

allCTAs.name: View Scores
allCTAs.description: Navigate to the student's score history across Star, DnA, and other assessments.

allCTAs.name: View Skill Proficiency
allCTAs.description: See which skills the student has mastered, is developing, or needs support with.

allCTAs.name: Enroll in Class
allCTAs.description: Add the student to a class roster so they receive class-level assignments and assessments.

allCTAs.name: Remove from Class
allCTAs.description: Remove the student from a class roster, ending their access to class-level content.

allCTAs.name: Add to Student Group
allCTAs.description: Add the student to an intervention or enrichment group for targeted instruction.

allCTAs.name: Export Data
allCTAs.description: Download the student's data as a file for compliance reporting or external analysis.

allCTAs.name: Transfer (School)
allCTAs.description: Move the student to a different school within the district, preserving all historical data.

allCTAs.name: Deactivate
allCTAs.description: Mark the student as inactive, removing them from active rosters while retaining historical data.

structure.evidence: A Student has a rich set of attributes: name, grade level, student ID, date of birth, enrollment date, demographic data, reading level, math level, and more. You can design a detail page for a Student.

instances.evidence: Jane Doe (3rd grader at Lincoln Elementary), Marcus Johnson (7th grader at Washington Middle School), Priya Patel (Kindergartener at Sunset Academy). Every school has hundreds or thousands of Student instances.

purpose.evidence: Students are the reason Renaissance products exist. Teachers view Student profiles, track Student progress, assign Student work, and analyze Student scores. Students themselves log in to complete assignments, take assessments, and read books.

sipValidation.verdict: Core system object. The universal anchor across all Renaissance products.

synonyms.notes: Used in "Learner Engine" and "Learning Path" contexts
Used when the audience is parents/guardians

objectViews.description: A scannable list of every student in a class with at-a-glance performance levels. Teachers use this to spot who needs attention and jump to individual profiles or scores.
objectViews.userIntent: Who is in my class and how are they performing?

objectViews.description: Students participating in a live class activity, shown as cards for quick visual identification. Teachers tap through to see what each student has been working on.
objectViews.userIntent: Which students are engaged in this class activity?

objectViews.description: The complete student profile — identity, enrollment, demographics, and academic performance in one place. Teachers and admins come here to check standing, manage enrollment, or drill into scores and skill proficiency.
objectViews.userIntent: What is the complete picture of this student?

objectViews.description: A minimal selection list for choosing which students receive an assignment. Shows just enough to identify each student — name, grade, and photo.
objectViews.userIntent: Which students should I assign this to?

objectViews.description: All students at a school listed with name, grade, and school affiliation. Principals and admins browse enrollment and jump to profiles or score summaries.
objectViews.userIntent: Who are the students at this school?

objectViews.description: Students matching a search query, displayed as compact cards with school affiliation. The goal is fast identification — confirm the right student and open their profile.
objectViews.userIntent: Is this the student I'm looking for?

objectViews.description: Students listed alongside their scores for a specific assessment. Teachers scan the data rows to compare performance, spot proficiency bands, and identify who needs follow-up.
objectViews.userIntent: How did each student score on this assessment?

objectViews.description: Members of an intervention or enrichment group with reading and math levels visible. Teachers check group composition and performance to decide on next steps.
objectViews.userIntent: Who is in this intervention/enrichment group?

objectViews.description: A parent's view of their child — a summary card showing grade level, reading and math performance, and recent activity. Designed for quick reassurance or to flag concerns.
objectViews.userIntent: How is my child doing overall?

lastLogin.description: When the student last logged in

objectViews.description: A header identifying which student's activity stream is being viewed, with recent activity count and last-active timestamp to contextualize the feed below.
objectViews.userIntent: What has this student been working on recently?

objectViews.description: Students listed against a specific assignment with individual progress, completion status, and scores. Teachers track who's finished, who's behind, and who needs help.
objectViews.userIntent: How is each student progressing on this assignment?

assignmentStatus.description: Status: Not Started, In Progress, Completed
